Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween

Class FlixelTweenManager

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.FlixelTweenManager
public class FlixelTweenManager extends Object
Manager class for handling a list of active FlixelTweens. normally used via FlixelTween.getGlobalManager() or the static helpers on FlixelTween (e.g. FlixelTween.updateTweens(float), FlixelTween.registerTweenType(java.lang.Class<T>, java.lang.Class<? extends me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.builders.FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T, ?>>, java.util.function.Supplier<? extends me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.builders.FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T, ?>>, java.util.function.Supplier<T>)) rather than instantiating separately. Adding a tween via addTween(FlixelTween) automatically starts it.

Uses a registry: each tween type is registered with its builder class and a pool factory. Only registered types can be used with FlixelTween.tween(Class, Class). Call clearPools() when clearing state (e.g. on state switch) to release pooled instances.

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelTweenManager

      public FlixelTweenManager()

  • Method Details

    • registerTweenType

      public <T extends FlixelTween> FlixelTweenManager registerTweenType(Class<T> tweenClass, Class<? extends FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T,?>> builderClass, Supplier<? extends FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T,?>> builderFactory, Supplier<T> poolFactory)
      Registers a tween type with its builder factory and a pool factory for creating new tween instances when the pool is empty. Register all tween types (including custom ones) before using FlixelTween.tween(Class, Class).
      Type Parameters:
      T - The tween type.
      Parameters:
      tweenClass - The tween class (e.g. FlixelPropertyTween.class).
      builderClass - The corresponding builder class, used for type verification when FlixelTween.tween(Class, Class) is called.
      builderFactory - A no-arg supplier that creates a fresh builder instance. Using an explicit factory avoids reflective newInstance() calls that fail on platforms without full reflection support (for example TeaVM).
      poolFactory - A supplier that creates a new tween instance when the pool is empty.
      Returns:
      The same manager, for chaining.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tween type is already registered.

    • getBuilderClass

      public Class<?> getBuilderClass(Class<? extends FlixelTween> tweenClass)
      Returns the builder class registered for the given tween type.
      Parameters:
      tweenClass - The registered tween class to look up.
      Returns:
      The registered builder class.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tween type is not registered.

    • createBuilder

      public <T extends FlixelTween, B extends FlixelAbstractTweenBuilder<T, B>> B createBuilder(Class<T> tweenType)
      Creates a new builder instance for the given tween type using the registered builder factory. This avoids reflective getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance() calls that are incompatible with platforms lacking full reflection support (for example TeaVM/web).
      Type Parameters:
      T - The tween type.
      B - The builder type.
      Parameters:
      tweenType - The tween class whose builder should be created.
      Returns:
      A new builder instance, cast to the requested builder type.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tween type is not registered.

    • addTween

      public FlixelTween addTween(FlixelTween tween)
      Adds the tween to this manager and starts it immediately.
      Parameters:
      tween - The tween to add and start.
      Returns:
      The same tween, for chaining.

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Updates all active tweens that are stored and updated in this manager.

      Iterates in reverse so that finished ONESHOT tweens can be removed by index without skipping elements or traversing null padding beyond the array's valid size.

      Parameters:
      elapsed - The amount of time that has passed since the last frame.

    • obtainTween

      public <T extends FlixelTween> T obtainTween(Class<T> type, Supplier<T> factory)
      Obtains a tween of the given type from the registry's pool. The returned instance is reset; the caller must set FlixelTween.setTweenSettings(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.settings.FlixelTweenSettings) and any type-specific state (for example FlixelPropertyTween.setObject(Object)) before addTween(FlixelTween). The factory is only used when the type is not registered; registered types ignore the supplier and use the pool's newObject() method.
      Parameters:
      type - The tween class (e.g. FlixelPropertyTween.class).
      factory - Fallback factory when the type is not registered or the pool is empty.
      Returns:
      A reset tween of type T, either from the pool or from factory.

    • removeTween

      public FlixelTween removeTween(FlixelTween tween, boolean destroy)
      Remove an FlixelTween from this manager. When destroy is true, the tween is reset and returned to its type's pool if registered.
      Parameters:
      tween - The tween to remove.
      destroy - If true, reset the tween and free it to the pool (if its type is registered).
      Returns:
      The removed tween.

    • getPool

      public com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool<FlixelTween> getPool(Class<? extends FlixelTween> tweenClass)

    • clearPools

      public void clearPools()

    • getActiveTweens

      public com.badlogic.gdx.utils.SnapshotArray<FlixelTween> getActiveTweens()

    • cancelTweensOf

      public void cancelTweensOf(Object object, String... fieldPaths)
      Cancels all active tweens matching object and optional field paths (OR semantics). When fieldPaths is empty, matches any tween FlixelTween.isTweenOf(Object, String) on object with a null/empty field.
      Parameters:
      object - Non-null root instance (same as passed to FlixelTween.isTweenOf(Object, String)).
      fieldPaths - Optional goal keys or dotted paths; empty means match all fields on object.

    • completeTweensOf

      public void completeTweensOf(@NotNull @NotNull Object object, String... fieldPaths)
      Completes matching tweens in one step (large delta). Non-looping tweens only. FlixelTweenSettings.getOnComplete() runs when FlixelTween.finish() is invoked after the tween reports finished.
      Parameters:
      object - The object to complete tweens of.
      fieldPaths - The field paths to complete tweens of.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the object is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - If the object is null.

    • completeAll

      public void completeAll()
      Completes all active non-looping tweens.

    • completeTweensOfType

      public void completeTweensOfType(Class<? extends FlixelTween> type)
      Completes all active tweens assignable to type (non-looping only).
      Parameters:
      type - The type of tween to complete.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the type is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - If the type is null.

    • containsTweensOf

      public boolean containsTweensOf(Object object, String... fieldPaths)
      Returns whether any active tween matches object and optional fieldPaths (OR).
      Parameters:
      object - The object to check for tweens of.
      fieldPaths - The field paths to check for tweens of.
      Returns:
      True if the manager contains tweens of the given object and field paths, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the object is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - If the object is null.

    • forEach

      public void forEach(Consumer<FlixelTween> action)
      Invokes action for each active tween (current snapshot order).
      Parameters:
      action - The action to invoke for each active tween.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the action is null.

    • resetRegistry

      public void resetRegistry()
      Clears the registry of all registered tween types and their respective pools.

      It is advised to only call this if you know what you are doing, as this will include the default registered tween types. If you call this, you will need to register the tween types again.
